MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-20-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANTS TIMOTHY REMPEL & REMPEL ARCHITECTURE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. SEE PRICE V. DAMES & MOORE (2001) 92 CAL.APP.4TH 355, 359-360. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO FILE AND SERVE A CERTIFICATE OF MERIT AND FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT PLEADING COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 411.35. PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT ARISES FROM PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE AND REQUIRING THE CERTIFICATE IN THE INSTANT CIRCUMSTANCES WILL FURTHER THE PURPOSE OF CCP 411.35. THE COURT SIGNED THE ORDER THAT WAS EMAILED TO THE COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-20-2013 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTs TIMOTHY REMPEL & REMPEL ARCHITECTURE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. See Price v. Dames & Moore (2001) 92 Cal.App.4th 355, 359-360. Plaintiff has leave to file and serve a certificate of merit and file an amended complaint pleading compliance with CCP 411.35. Plaintiff's complaint arises from professional negligence and requiring the certificate in the instant circumstances will further the purpose of CCP 411.35. The Court signed the order that was emailed to the court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
